summary: Iteration 9 validates the Netlify blank-page fix: (1) deletion of /app/frontend/public/_redirects so netlify.toml is the sole redirect source, and (2) defensive type guards in useSiteData.js that prevent React from crashing when /api/* returns non-JSON (HTML). All static, backend, and frontend regression + defensive checks are GREEN.

static_checks.public_redirects_deleted: PASS — ls /app/frontend/public/_redirects → 'No such file or directory'.
static_checks.netlify_toml_ordering: PASS — 9 /api/* redirects all with `status = 200, force = true` precede the final `/*  -> /index.html` SPA catchall.
static_checks.useSiteData_defensive_checks: PASS — useSiteSettings only calls setSettings when `d && typeof d === 'object' && !Array.isArray(d)`; useVideos only calls setVideos when `Array.isArray(r?.data)`. Both hooks .catch(() => {}) and fall back to DEFAULT_SETTINGS / empty array.
static_checks.yarn_build: PASS — cd /app/frontend && rm -rf build && yarn build → 'Compiled successfully', 217.49 kB gzipped main JS. Build output contains index.html, static/, asset-manifest.json and NO _redirects file (confirmed via ls /app/frontend/build/).

backend_regression: PASS — 13/13 pytest cases against Emergent FastAPI preview (admin login Ashutosh12/Ashutosh@12, /api/admin/me with & without token, PUT /api/admin/settings, videos full CRUD + auth guard, POST /api/bookings valid + invalid email, GET /api/admin/bookings contains new booking, old-password rejected). Report: /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml.

frontend_regression.landing_render: PASS — https://atman-events.preview.emergentagent.com/ loads Hero (ATMAN heading, tagline, subline), Genre badges (BOLLYWOOD/SUFI/ROCK), Book/Watch CTAs, and nav (HOME/ABOUT/SERVICES/GENRES/VIDEOS/GALLERY/REVIEWS/CONTACT). body.innerText length = 3992. Zero pageerror events, zero console errors.
frontend_regression.admin_login_and_dashboard: PASS — /admin login with Ashutosh12/Ashutosh@12 → redirects to /admin/dashboard, shows 'Welcome back.' toast, Contact/Videos/Inquiries tabs render, contact form pre-populated from persisted state (TEST_ email, Mumbai Test location, tagline/subline).
frontend_regression.defensive_html_injection: PASS — Playwright route.fulfill forced /api/settings and /api/videos to return `<!DOCTYPE html>...</html>` with content-type text/html. Landing page STILL rendered all sections (ATMAN hero visible, BOOK ATMAN CTA visible, body.innerText length = 4034), zero page errors. Confirms the type-guard fallback works and t.map crash is prevented.

critical_code_review_comments: Defensive guards in useSiteData.js are correct and minimal — good pattern. Consider extending the same pattern to any future hook consuming /api/* to make the frontend fully tolerant of Netlify redirect misconfigurations.

Since netlify.toml is now the sole redirect source, add a CI guard (or a pre-commit hook) to fail if `frontend/public/_redirects` ever reappears; this documented Netlify precedence rule can silently reintroduce the blank-page bug.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (landing render, admin login, defensive HTML injection all pass)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Netlify blank-page RCA fully resolved locally: /app/frontend/public/_redirects removed, useSiteData.js hardened with type guards, yarn build produces a build folder without any _redirects file, so netlify.toml is authoritative on the live deploy. Final verification requires a Netlify redeploy — after deploy, curl https://atman-music.netlify.app/api/settings should return JSON (not HTML), and the site should render.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Netlify applies `publish_dir/_redirects` BEFORE `netlify.toml` [[redirects]]. The old `frontend/public/_redirects` contained `/*  /index.html 200`, which was copied into build/ by CRA and matched /api/* first, so /api/settings and /api/videos returned index.html (HTML string). Performances.jsx then called `.map` on that string → 't.map is not a function' → whole React tree crashed → black screen. Fix (both applied and verified): (1) delete frontend/public/_redirects so build/ contains no _redirects and netlify.toml wins; (2) harden useSiteData.js with typeof-object and Array.isArray checks so even a future non-JSON response degrades gracefully to defaults instead of crashing.
